Food sits at the intersection of health, agriculture, economy and culture. And hospitality is where these worlds meet every day.

Christian Hamerle invites us to rethink the role of food and hospitality as catalysts for healthier people, stronger ecosystems and a more sustainable economy.

This is exactly the kind of systemic thinking that BEAM brings to the conversation on the future of hospitality.

It was a pleasure having @foodservicelab  and @dussmann_italy  as partner again this year.
 
#beamsummit #hospitality #innovation

Alongside the vision shared by our partner @hgv__suedtirol , this is exactly what emerged at the BEAM Summit in Bolzano. 
Over the past days, collaboration has proven to be more than just a theme, it has been a concrete way of working, exchanging ideas, and rethinking the future of the industry together.

Because it is only by connecting perspectives and turning insights into action that hospitality can truly evolve toward a more human, sustainable, and forward-looking direction.

Sono stati 2 giorni molto belli e stimolanti quelli passati a @beam_summit a Bolzano! 
L’ospitalità è un settore che cambia ed evolve sempre più velocemente e per restare aggiornata non basta solo studiare dati e numeri, né passare le giornate a chiedersi dove stiamo andando e quali saranno i desideri degli ospiti del futuro.

L’unica vera soluzione, per me, è il confronto con altri professionisti del settore: ognuno, con la propria esperienza, porta nuove idee e un arricchimento che nessun libro, nessuna analisi dati può dare. 

Sono tornata a casa con tante idee da applicare al mio lavoro ♥️

@beam_summit 
#adv #beamsummit

„Lead the Shift!“ – das Motto der @beam_summit bringt auf den Punkt, was in der @fieramesse in Bozen spürbar war 🚀
Zwei Tage voller Impulse, Diskussionen und Austausch rund um die Zukunft der Hospitality: Über 300 Teilnehmende und mehr als 35 Speaker zeigten, wie stark sich die Branche aktuell im Wandel befindet – und wie konkret neue Ansätze bereits umgesetzt werden.
 
👀 Vor Ort 
Unsere Redaktion war in Bozen dabei und hat zentrale Themen, Stimmungen und Impulse direkt aus den Sessions und Gesprächen mitgenommen.
 
🤝 Haltung als Differenzierungsfaktor
Im Zentrum vieler Beiträge stand die Frage nach klarer Positionierung. Denn Gastfreundschaft entsteht nicht nur durch operative Exzellenz, sondern zunehmend durch ein klares Profil und konsistente Konzepte.
 
🏔️ Austausch & Perspektiven
Ob im Zukunftslabor, bei Panels oder dem Networking auf @maretsch_mareccio : Der Fokus lag auf praxisnahen Ideen, neuen Denkansätzen und dem offenen Dialog innerhalb der Branche.
 
Der Tenor vor Ort: Der Wandel ist längst Realität – und viele Betriebe arbeiten bereits konkret an der Umsetzung.🏨🔥

Due giorni a Bolzano per il BEAM Hospitality Game Changer Summit, un evento che riunisce professionisti e innovatori del mondo dell’hospitality per immaginare insieme il futuro del settore.

Speech, workshop e momenti di confronto che hanno aperto riflessioni su come sta evolvendo il modo di vivere l’ospitalità tra nuove generazioni, tecnologia e sostenibilità — non solo ambientale, ma anche sociale.

Un’esperienza ricca di stimoli, connessioni e nuove prospettive, in cui il filo conduttore è stato chiaro: riportare sempre tutto a una dimensione più umana.

50+ national tourism associations in Germany. 
50 presidents. 50 agendas. 50 different voices.

Result? Zero political relevance.

Michael Otremba called it out at BEAM: "During COVID, the transport minister met with the automotive industry every week. With tourism? Never."

Travel is a primal human aspiration. The first books ever written were travel reports. The Odyssey itself is one long journey.

Yet as an industry, we're invisible. 
Why? Because we can't agree on anything except overnight numbers.

"I understand every minister who says: clarify your opinion first, then come back to me." 
Seven years later, nothing happens.

It's time to stop being comfortable.

BEAM 2026 | May 19-20 | Bolzano

Fair question.

- Random networking

The 5-Trillion-Dollar Business Case – Food as the next big thing after the internet

At this year's @beam_summit  in Bolzano, the Dussmann Group will be supporting an interactive masterclass that will explore why sustainability is becoming an increasingly powerful driver of revenue for the food sector. 

This session highlights the specific action areas that are now open to decision-makers in the hospitality industry, food service, agriculture and healthcare.

The masterclass will be hosted by Christian Hamerle, Head of Global Food Service Transformation at Dussmann Group, together with an outstanding panel of experts including Jörg Reuter, Christian Sina, Roger A. Jann and Tilo Hühn.

📅 Tuesday, 19 May 2026 | approx. 16:15–18:00
🎟️ Get your ticket and join us in Bolzano: Link in Bio [https://beam.fierabolzano.it/de/tickets/] 

We look forward to meeting you in Bolzano to discuss the future of food services.

Photo: @beam_summit  @fieramesse

9,000 residents. 4.5 million visitors.
Immense traffic. Trail degradation. Human-wildlife conflicts.
Banff National Park was breaking.

Leslie Bruce did something radical: she invited their biggest regulators and harshest critics to build the future together.

Federal government, local government, and business community at the same table. 

2,000 people surveyed. 550+ ideas generated.

The result?
2M+ people on mass transit. Indigenous showcases supported. A community that went from adversaries to allies.

The secret?
Finding the one thing everyone agreed on: we all love this park.

See you at BEAM 2026? 
May 19-20 | Bolzano

Everyone talks about real transformation.
But what does it really mean in hospitality?

Boris Lebedev (Speaker, Business Consultant, transformative facilitator) is one of the speakers at BEAM 2026 – and he’ll explore what a regenerative hospitality economy is…and what it isn’t.

REAL transformation is…
 Not superficial change.
 Not small adjustments.
 Not just marketing.

It’s an energetic stance.
 A different way of relating to life.
 A different way of doing business.
 Painful, risky – AND worth it.

With over 10 years in the industry and roles in Reinventing Society and Club of Rome Germany, Boris brings a clear perspective on how hospitality can move towards a regenerative future.

At BEAM, discover what that shift could look like.

"The future we want must be invented - otherwise we get one we don’t want.” 

Building on the spirit of Joseph Beuys, Sabine Rogg, Managing Director Avantgarde Germany & Trendbüro, takes the stage at the BEAM Summit to explore the Trends to Watch 2026.

With a meta-mood for 2026 moving at full speed in a two-way dynamic, her keynote dives into the relevant drivers reshaping consumer mindsets – and what they mean for brand relevance.

At BEAM, she will share how overarching, ongoing shifts influence expectations, values, and decision-making – and how brands can actively shape the future instead of reacting to it. Because strong brands don’t wait for what’s next. They curate and guide future directions.

We're consuming 1.7 Earths. By 2050, it'll be three planets.

Diane Verde Nieto didn't come to BEAM to depress us.
She came to show us the way forward.

Hospitality represents 10% of global GDP and touches every system: food, energy, culture, community, wellness.
When you change hospitality, the ripple effect is massive.

But sustainability isn't enough anymore. We need regeneration.

Her work in Tismoudin, Morocco, transformed an abandoned village into a thriving community in three years. 

How? By asking one question: "What are your dreams?"

Intention matters.

BEAM 2026 | May 19-20 | Bolzano

"Wellness hotel. Coworking space. Community hub.

What if you didn't have to choose?

@magnus.berlin (Founder nook society) is one of the speakers at BEAM 2026.

And he created something that didn't exist before: EXPERIENCE TRAVEL, a new segment in hospitality that combines wellness, new work, and community in a single business model.

But Magnus isn't just a visionary.
He's an operator who transforms underperforming properties into sought-after destinations through asset-light models, strategic repositioning, and innovative targeting.

From boutique hotels to wellness retreats, from member-managed spaces to places where culture and social life meet – nook society proves that hospitality can be reinvented.

At BEAM, he shares how he created a market from scratch and made properties profitable that others would have sold.

"Medical prescription: 7 days in Sweden"

Sweden is the first destination in the world to promote stays on medical prescription.

Nils Persson (CMO Visit Sweden) coordinated "The Swedish Prescription" – the campaign that transformed destination marketing from "come see" to "come feel better".

Not a slogan. Scientifically proven effects on physical and mental health.

At BEAM, discover how bold marketing generates results that best practices will never reach.

Face-to-face interactions have dropped by 30% in the last decade. 
Teens don't go out anymore. They stay home.

We're living in what Philipp Kirnbauer calls the "Anti-Social Century" — an era where we've lost the Circle of Village. 

That space where you meet people who disagree with you. 
Where you learn tolerance. 
Where serendipity happens.
Technology amplifies the I, not the WE.

And nothing truly wonderful will ever happen to you on a screen.

The solution? 
It's not another app. It's physical. Spatial. Human.

At BEAM 2025, we explored how to design spaces that rebuild the connections we've lost. Spaces that create the conditions for the Circle of Village to exist again.

Because in 2026, people don't choose who serves them better. 
They choose who makes them feel something.

BEAM 2026 | May 19-20 | Bolzano
